growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: L-lactate (10 mM) supports growth as major carbon source via lactate permease LctP and lactate dehydrogenase; consumption not numerically quantified in main text (growth by CFU, figure only)

growth_records.cite: Sinha et al. Gut metabolite L-lactate supports Campylobacter jejuni population expansion during acute infection. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2024). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2316540120. [PMC10786315]
growth_records.snippet: WT strain in minimal media containing mucin with and without butyrate (10 mM) and L-lactate (10 mM) over 48 h was determined by CFU counts (n = 3) ... observed better growth with L-lactate in the media than with butyrate
growth_records.notes: C. jejuni 11168 grows in mucin minimal media with 10 mM L-lactate (better than 10 mM butyrate); growth measured by CFU/mL over 48 h (Fig 4). No numeric growth rate, doubling time, or specific uptake rate given in the main text (values only in figures; media recipe and culture conditions in SI Appendix, not in fulltext XML), so no rate could be computed. Cecal L-lactate concentration reported as 4.2 ± 2.9 mmol/kg (host environment, not a culture parameter). Record captures the qualitative growth-supporting L-lactate carbon source.
